primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign currency risk arising from intra-group dividend receivables recognized in consolidated financial statements can be designated as a hedged item under hedge accounting rules

primary_authority.reason: Governs hedge accounting eligibility criteria, including which items can be designated as hedged items and restrictions on intragroup items
Addresses recognition and treatment of exchange differences on monetary items in consolidated financial statements, including intragroup monetary items

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies the scope of hedged risk in net investment hedges and the restriction to functional currency differences between parent and foreign operation

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses insurance contract measurement but does not govern hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on dividend receivables
Disclosures for financing activities; not relevant to hedge accounting eligibility determination
Provisions and contingent liabilities framework; not relevant to hedge accounting for monetary items

treatment_families.family: Net investment hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items
Fair value hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on monetary items
No hedge accounting permitted for intragroup items

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21.32 permits OCI treatment for exchange differences on monetary items forming part of net investment; IFRIC 16 confirms hedge accounting may apply to foreign currency differences between functional currencies of parent and foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits fair value hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on monetary items; may apply to intragroup dividend receivables if structured appropriately

approaches.label: No hedge accounting (intragroup restriction applies)
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9.6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to transactions with parties external to the reporting entity, which may preclude hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivables
